senses.definition: the 24th letter of the Roman alphabet

examples.text: The farmer signed with an x.

senses.definition: a radiogram made by exposing photographic film to X rays
electromagnetic radiation of short wavelength produced when high-speed electrons strike a solid target
take an x-ray of something or somebody

examples.text: The doctor x-rayed my chest

senses.definition: a salt or ester of xanthic acid
excess carotene in the blood stream

examples.text: Eating too many carrots had contributed to the child's xanthemia.

senses.definition: crystalline oxidation product of the metabolism of nucleoproteins

examples.text: Once generated from guanine, xanthine is often turned into uric acid.

senses.definition: a skin problem marked by the development of irregular yellow nodules on the eyelids, neck and back

examples.text: The patient is suffering from xanthoma.

senses.definition: widespread xanthomas (especially on elbows and knees)

examples.text: I cultured a flask full of xanthomonad.

senses.definition: yellow carotenoid pigments in plants and animal fats and egg yolks
yellow carotenoid pigments in plants and animal fats and egg yolks
visual defect in which objects appear to have a yellowish hue

examples.text: The drug digitalin is thought to cause xanthopsia.

senses.definition: an abnormal yellow discoloration of the skin

examples.text: The child was so unhealthy that the skin displayed xanthosis.

senses.definition: of the color intermediate between green and orange in the color spectrum

examples.text: The new rug you bought is fairly xanthous, don't you think?
It's my xcvii birthday, Augustus!

senses.definition: denoting or relating to cells or tissues from individuals belonging to different species

examples.text: The xenogenic reactions of trees and mosses is quite fascinating.
examples.translation: 树木和苔藓之间的异种反应相当令人着迷。

senses.definition: the production of offspring that is distinct from either parent

examples.text: The two creatures did not look alike, but were thought to be different stages in xenogenesis.
examples.translation: 这两种生物看起来并不相像，但被认为是异源生殖过程中的不同阶段。

senses.definition: tissue from an animal of one species used as a temporary graft, as in cases of severe burns, on an individual of another species

examples.text: The surgeons used skin from a pig as a xenograft.
examples.translation: 外科医生使用猪皮作为异种移植物。

senses.definition: a piece of rock of different origin from the igneous rock in which it is embedded
a colorless, odorless, inert gaseous element occurring in the earth's atmosphere in trace amounts

examples.text: The canister was filled with xenon.

senses.definition: a fear of foreigners or strangers

examples.text: I think your xenophobia might be cured by a trip abroad.
examples.translation: 我觉得你的仇外心理可以通过一次出国旅行得到改善。

senses.definition: suffering from xenophobia, or the fear of outsiders

examples.text: Those remarks make it pretty clear how xenophobic you are.
examples.translation: 这些言论非常清楚地表明你有多么仇外。

senses.definition: a brown-to-yellow mineral that is a phosphate of yttrium in crystalline form
a surgical procedure in which tissue or whole organs are transfered from one species to another species
a surgical procedure in which tissue or whole organs are transfered from one species to another species

examples.text: The comparable sizes of organs in pigs and humans means that xenotransplantation could be feasible.
examples.translation: 猪和人类器官尺寸相近，这意味着异种移植术可能是可行的。

examples.text: The Sahara desert is a xeric environment – it rarely rains there.
examples.translation: 撒哈拉沙漠是干旱环境——那里很少下雨。

senses.definition: a mild form of ichthyosis characterized by abnormal dryness and roughness of the skin
a mild form of ichthyosis characterized by abnormal dryness and roughness of the skin
of or relating to copying paper or other items, aka xerography

examples.text: Our xerographic machine was on the fritz.
examples.translation: 我们的静电复印机出故障了。

senses.definition: forming an image by the action of light on a specially coated charged plate

examples.text: The machine used xerography to print copies.
examples.translation: 这台机器使用静电复印术打印复印件。

senses.definition: abnormal dryness of the conjunctiva and cornea of the eyes

examples.text: Your eyes have a condition called xeroma.
examples.translation: 你的眼睛得了一种叫干眼病的病。

senses.definition: plant adapted for life with a limited supply of water

examples.text: Cacti are some of the most well-known xerophiles.
examples.translation: 仙人掌是一些最广为人知的喜旱植物。

senses.definition: abnormal dryness of the conjunctiva and cornea of the eyes
abnormal dryness of the conjunctiva and cornea of the eyes
plant adapted for life with a limited supply of water

examples.text: The gardener loved the easy maintenance of the xerophyte.
examples.translation: 园丁喜欢这种旱生植物易于养护的特性。

examples.text: Cacti are xerophytic plants.

senses.definition: radiography using X-rays and xerographic techniques

examples.text: The radiologist specialized in xeroradiography.
examples.translation: 这位放射科医生专门研究干静电摄影法。

senses.definition: abnormal dryness of the mouth resulting from decreased secretion of saliva
the condition of not containing or being covered by a liquid, especially water

examples.text: The xerotes of the skin was helped by applying a wax.
examples.translation: 涂抹蜡质有助于改善皮肤干燥。

senses.definition: a duplicator (trade mark Xerox) that copies graphic matter by the action of light on an electrically charged photoconductive insulating surface in which the latent image is developed with a resinous powder
a copy made by a xerographic printer
the 14th letter of the Greek alphabet
senses.translation: 希腊字母表的第14个字母

senses.definition: the woody part of plants: the supporting and water-conducting tissue, consisting primarily of tracheids and vessels

examples.text: The xylem carries water up into the tree.

senses.definition: a colorless flammable volatile liquid hydrocarbon used as a solvent
a colorless flammable volatile liquid hydrocarbon used as a solvent

examples.text: The only way the powder would dissolve was in xylol.

senses.definition: a percussion instrument with wooden bars tuned to produce a chromatic scale, with resonators to help with the sound quality

examples.text: The children were encouraged to play the xylophone.
The xylophonist played the Danse Macabre.

senses.definition: a sugar extracted from wood or straw

examples.text: Xylose is found in most plants that humans consume.
